evals.prompt: I've exported June's transactions from my Chatori Finance app (chatori-june.csv). Compile my June P&L properly like an accountant would and tell me straight — did I actually make money in June?
assertions: Excludes the Rs 30,000 customer advance from earned revenue and reports it as a liability / held-not-earned line
Counts Sandeep's June salary expense as Rs 25,000 (20,000 cash + 5,000 advance adjusted), not Rs 20,000
Excludes the Rs 5,000 staff advance to Neeraj from expenses (treats it as a receivable)
Separates COGS from operating expenses and reports food cost %, flagging it is above 40%
Concludes June was a LOSS on an adjusted basis (approx -Rs 5,800), not a profit
Ends with a plain-language owner summary

evals.prompt: A customer just paid me Rs 50,000 advance for a wedding I'm catering in September. How should this show up in my books and in my monthly report?
assertions: States the advance is unearned revenue / a liability, not income of the month received
Says it becomes revenue only when the wedding is delivered (September / on completion)
Gives practical recording steps for the app (Customer Advance category, party name, link to the event)
Explains how the monthly report presents it (held/unearned line, excluded from earned revenue)

evals.prompt: It's 3rd August and I'm closing July's books for my catering business. I paid the staff their July salaries yesterday. What do I need to do, step by step?
assertions: Instructs to record/verify salaries against July (the month they are FOR), not August (payment date)
Includes reconciling cash and UPI/bank balances against actual balances
Covers customer advances: map to events and recognize delivered events as revenue
Produces or commits to an adjusted P&L plus a plain-language owner summary
